text: The surgeon uses robotic instruments to carefully manipulate and tease the cystic duct, attempting to achieve the critical view of safety. Despite toggling between standard and immunofluorescence imaging, the cystic duct and biliary tree do not illuminate with indocyanine green. The surgeon performs short cautery applications on crossing vessels and continues to milk the duct, observing no stones present.
The surgeon isolates the cystic artery, visually inspecting for stones but finding none.
The surgeon uses round tip scissors without an energy attachment to carefully dissect the tissue from the cystic plate.
